theme: Restructure the Python IR analyzer (tywrap_ir) — capture dropped member categories, fix IRCache, single-source version, add pytest IR/protocol suites, and retire the redundant TS-side analyzer
verdict: Worth doing, with one important refinement to the expert review's framing. The core defect is real and I confirmed it empirically: a type-safe wrapper generator that silently omits @property is incomplete by its own definition (F1/F2). But the review over-states the @classmethod/@staticmethod part — those members ARE captured (they pass inspect.ismethoddescriptor on 3.10+), they are just MISLABELED as ordinary methods and lose their cls/self semantics and any kind marker. The truly dropped categories are @property and @overload alternatives. I recommend: (1) do the member-category capture (high value, medium effort) — this is the load-bearing fix; (2) do the IRCache None fix, version single-sourcing, and pytest suites (all small/cheap, high trust value); (3) RETIRE src/core/analyzer.ts (PyAnalyzer) entirely as part of the parallel-processor deletion — I confirmed it is reachable in production ONLY through the dead parallel-processor subsystem, and the real generate() path goes through fetchPythonIr -> python -m tywrap_ir. This removes the two highest-complexity TS functions (extractImports 27/92, extractParameters 23/85) at zero production cost and eliminates a divergent second source of truth. I would SCOPE DOWN the ir.py module split: the file is 716 lines and cohesive (one extractor with helpers); a split is cosmetic and not worth the churn/import-path breakage unless the accessor work pushes it materially larger. Treat the split as optional.
verifiedSignals: F1 CONFIRMED + REFINED: ir.py:482-485 predicate is `lambda x: inspect.isfunction(x) or inspect.ismethoddescriptor(x) or inspect.isbuiltin(x)`. Empirically (Python 3.14): for a class with @property/@classmethod/@staticmethod/regular methods, the predicate captures ['regular','util'] and DROPS 'value' (the property). property objects fail all three predicate branches (isfunction=False, ismethoddescriptor=False, isbuiltin=False), so @property is silently dropped.

F1 REFINEMENT (classmethod/staticmethod NOT dropped): @classmethod 'make' and @staticmethod 'util' both pass inspect.ismethoddescriptor on 3.10+, so they ARE in the output — but _extract_function (ir.py:408-468) emits no classmethod/staticmethod marker and inspect.signature on the bound classmethod shows params ['x'] (cls already bound), so they render as ordinary instance methods. The bug is mislabeling/lost semantics, not omission.

F2 CONFIRMED: ir.py never calls typing.get_overloads. _extract_class iterates inspect.getmembers which surfaces an @overload-decorated method only ONCE (the final implementation). Empirically typing.get_overloads(Calc.add) returns 2 overloads while getmembers sees 'add' a single time — every overload alternative is lost.

IRCache None-bug CONFIRMED: optimized_ir.py:108-109 `result = self.get(key); if result is not None: return result`. _extract_function returns Optional[IRFunction] (None when inspect.signature raises, common for many builtins) and _extract_class returns Optional[IRClass] (None for private/unnamed classes). set() at :113 stores None, but get() returning None is indistinguishable from a miss, so None-yielding members are recomputed on every call. Output is still correct; it is a cache-effectiveness bug (matches the review's 'None-returning extractions actually cache' fix).

B5 CONFIRMED (analyzer.ts redundant): grep shows PyAnalyzer/analyzePythonModule imported in production ONLY by src/utils/parallel-processor.ts:15,790-850 (itself the dead subsystem slated for deletion in B1) plus test/analyzer.test.ts, two benchmarks, and a .skip.ts. The real codegen path is generate() (tywrap.ts:123) -> fetchPythonIr (tywrap.ts:350,371) which spawns `python -m tywrap_ir --module ... --ir-version ... --no-pretty` and JSON.parses the result, then transformIrToTsModel (tywrap.ts:463). The tree-sitter analyzer never runs in generate().

ts-fx complexity hotspots CONFIRMED as belonging to the redundant analyzer: extractImports lives at analyzer.ts:561 (cyc/cog 27/92) and extractParameters at analyzer.ts:284 (23/85). Both parse Python-shaped tree-sitter nodes that the Python IR already produces structurally — they are reimplementing in TS what inspect/typing give for free in Python.

F4 CONFIRMED (version drift): IR_VERSION is hardcoded as '0.2.0' in __init__.py:8, ir.py:663 & :707 defaults, __main__.py:13 default, optimized_ir.py:161/414, and mirrored as TYWRAP_IR_VERSION='0.2.0' at src/tywrap.ts:25. __version__='0.2.1' duplicated in __init__.py:7 and pyproject.toml:7. All hand-synced literals.

Member flow gap CONFIRMED in TS consumer: transformIrToTsModel.mapClass (tywrap.ts:544-582) reads only c.methods and c.fields; there is no accessors/overloads field, so even if Python emitted them today they would be dropped at the TS boundary. The PythonClass TS type (types/index.ts:31) has methods: PythonFunction[] and a kind union but no accessor concept.

Existing Python test coverage CONFIRMED thin (F3): only tywrap_ir/tests/{test_ir_async_generator_flags.py, test_optimized_ir_options.py, test_optimized_ir_parallel_warnings.py} and test/python/test_safe_codec.py. No dispatch_request/protocol tests, no IR fixtures for property/classmethod/overload/dataclass/TypedDict/namedtuple/pydantic.
falsePositives: REVIEW F1 over-states the drop: it says '@property/@classmethod dropped'. Empirically @classmethod and @staticmethod are NOT dropped (they pass inspect.ismethoddescriptor on 3.10+); they are captured but MISLABELED as ordinary methods with lost cls/self semantics and no kind marker. Only @property is genuinely omitted. The fix must distinguish 'capture the missing properties' from 'correctly tag the already-captured classmethods/staticmethods'.

REVIEW frames the IRCache bug as data loss; it is not. The produced IR is correct — None-returning extractions simply never get served from cache and are recomputed. It is a cache-effectiveness/perf bug. (Also note: optimized_ir.py is part of the same lightly-used subsystem — extract_module_ir_optimized is not on the generate() path, which only calls `python -m tywrap_ir` -> emit_ir_json -> extract_module_ir, the NON-optimized path. So this bug currently has near-zero production impact and the fix is mostly hygiene.)

ts-fx 'deadcode' BLIND result correctly ignored: it returned zero findings (barrel masks dead code per digest). I proved analyzer.ts retirement via dynamic reachability (grep for callers), not via ts-fx's empty result.

The theme prompt's 'module split if ir.py is monolithic' — I refute the necessity. ir.py is 716 lines, single cohesive extractor with private helpers and clear sectioning (type-params, constants, aliases, function, class, metadata). A split is cosmetic, breaks the optimized_ir.py import block (it imports 8 names from .ir), and buys little. Recommend deferring/skipping unless accessor work materially grows the file.
proposal:
Target architecture for tywrap_ir and its TS boundary:

1) MEMBER CATEGORY CAPTURE (ir.py). Replace the single-predicate getmembers loop in _extract_class (ir.py:481-495) with an explicit per-member classification that walks cls.__dict__ across the MRO (or inspect.classify_class_attrs) and routes each member by its raw descriptor type:
   - property objects (isinstance(v, property)) -> IRAccessor with getter/setter/deleter presence + fget return annotation.
   - classmethod/staticmethod (isinstance(v, classmethod|staticmethod)) -> unwrap via __func__ before _extract_function, tag IRFunction.method_kind = 'classmethod'|'staticmethod'; strip the bound cls for classmethods so the signature is faithful.
   - functools.cached_property -> accessor (readonly).
   - regular function/methoddescriptor -> IRFunction.method_kind = 'instance' (default).
   - abstractmethod flag (getattr(v,'__isabstractmethod__',False)) -> IRFunction.is_abstract.
   New field IRFunction.method_kind: str (default 'instance') and IRFunction.is_abstract: bool. New dataclass IRAccessor(name, annotation, is_readonly, has_setter, has_deleter, docstring). New IRClass field accessors: List[IRAccessor].

2) OVERLOAD CAPTURE (ir.py). After resolving each method, call typing.get_overloads(func) (guarded: hasattr(typing,'get_overloads'), i.e. 3.11+; on 3.10 emit a single warning into IRModule.warnings). When overloads exist, attach IRFunction.overloads: List[IRSignature] (the alternative signatures) while keeping the implementation signature as the primary. Add a module-level minimum-Python note. Requires-python is already >=3.10 (pyproject.toml:18); get_overloads needs 3.11, so 3.10 degrades gracefully.

3) SINGLE-SOURCED VERSION (ir.py/__init__.py/__main__.py). Define IR_VERSION once (constant in a new tywrap_ir/_version.py or in __init__) and import it everywhere; make ir_version/_extract default to that constant instead of the literal '0.2.0'. Derive __version__ via importlib.metadata.version('tywrap-ir') with the hardcoded value as fallback. On the TS side, replace the hardcoded TYWRAP_IR_VERSION (tywrap.ts:25) with a value read from a generated/checked-in JSON or add a CI drift check that asserts it equals the Python IR_VERSION. Bump IR_VERSION (e.g. 0.3.0) because the IR schema gains fields.

4) TS BOUNDARY (tywrap.ts transformIrToTsModel + types). Extend PythonClass with accessors and extend mapClass (tywrap.ts:544) to map c.accessors -> Property entries (getter/setter/readonly from has_setter/has_deleter) and to propagate method_kind/is_abstract/overloads onto PythonFunction. generator.ts already emits TS overload families (generator.ts:432-494) — feed real Python overloads into that path rather than only the synthetic arg-count overloads. The built-in default-exclude set (tywrap.ts:198-205) already filters the decorator NAMES property/classmethod/staticmethod from module-level exports; that is unrelated and should stay, but ensure accessors inside a class are NOT excluded by it.

5) RETIRE src/core/analyzer.ts (PyAnalyzer). Delete analyzer.ts together with the parallel-processor deletion (B1). Remove test/analyzer.test.ts, test/benchmarks/{code_generation,ir_extraction}.bench.ts references, and the .skip.ts. tywrap_ir becomes the single authoritative analyzer. Document in CLAUDE.md that the authoritative analysis path is python -m tywrap_ir -> fetchPythonIr -> transformIrToTsModel, and that there is no tree-sitter fallback.

6) PYTEST SUITES (tywrap_ir/tests + test/python). Add IR fixture tests: a fixtures module with @property, @classmethod, @staticmethod, @overload, abstractmethod, dataclass, TypedDict (total/partial), NamedTuple, pydantic, type aliases, generics — assert the emitted IR shape. Add protocol tests against runtime/python_bridge.py dispatch_request (call/instantiate/call_method/dispose/meta, ProtocolError paths, bytes envelope decode, size limits). Add an IRCache regression test asserting None-returning extractions are cached via a _MISS sentinel.

7) IRCache fix (optimized_ir.py:88-114). Introduce a module-level _MISS = object() sentinel; get() returns _MISS on absence; cached_function checks `is not _MISS`. (Low production impact since the optimized path is not on generate(), but it is the documented fix and is cheap.)
breakingChanges: IR schema gains new fields (IRFunction.method_kind, IRFunction.is_abstract, IRFunction.overloads, IRClass.accessors, IRAccessor dataclass). Any external consumer of the raw JSON IR sees new keys; tolerant consumers are unaffected but the IR_VERSION bumps (0.2.0 -> 0.3.0), and the TS TYWRAP_IR_VERSION must move in lockstep or fetch will mismatch.

Generated TypeScript wrappers CHANGE: classes now emit property accessors (getters/setters) and classmethod/staticmethod as static members, plus real @overload families. Previously-generated wrapper files become out-of-date; `generate --check` will report drift and downstream snapshots/golden files must be regenerated.

@classmethod/@staticmethod methods that previously rendered as instance methods (callable as obj.make(...)) will now render with classmethod/staticmethod semantics (Class.make(...) / static). This is a call-shape change for any code already using the old generated output.

Removal of src/core/analyzer.ts (PyAnalyzer) and its public export from the barrel (if exported). Anyone importing PyAnalyzer from the package entry point breaks. Also removes the tree-sitter-python + tree-sitter dependency usage (can be dropped from dependencies if not used elsewhere — verify before removing from package.json).
Deletion of test/analyzer.test.ts and the two analyzer benchmarks; CI scripts that reference ir_extraction.bench.ts / code_generation.bench.ts must be updated.

Minimum Python for full overload support is effectively 3.11 (typing.get_overloads). On 3.10 overloads are silently degraded to the implementation signature plus a warning — a behavioral difference across the supported range that must be documented.
If ir.py is split into submodules (optional, not recommended), optimized_ir.py's `from .ir import (...)` block and any external `from tywrap_ir.ir import` break and need updating.
verificationPlan: Characterize-then-change. (1) Before edits, run `python -m tywrap_ir --module <fixture>` against a new fixtures module containing every member category and snapshot the current JSON IR — this documents exactly what is dropped (no property, classmethods unmarked, no overloads). (2) Add pytest IR-fixture tests asserting the NEW shape (properties present in accessors, classmethods marked, overloads listed); these tests fail before the ir.py change and pass after — proving the gap is closed. (3) Add a pytest protocol suite for runtime/python_bridge.py dispatch_request covering call/instantiate/call_method/dispose/meta + ProtocolError + bytes envelope + size-limit paths (currently zero coverage). (4) IRCache: a unit test that calls a cached_function whose wrapped fn returns None twice and asserts the underlying fn is invoked once (proves the _MISS sentinel works); fails before fix. (5) Version single-sourcing: a CI/pytest assertion that IR_VERSION (Python) == TYWRAP_IR_VERSION (TS) == the value emitted in IR metadata. (6) analyzer.ts retirement: grep proof of no remaining production callers after parallel-processor deletion; run `npm run build && npm run typecheck && npm test` to confirm nothing else imports PyAnalyzer; regenerate example wrappers (examples/living-app) and run the Python integration suites (test:python:suite:core/data) to confirm the generate() path still produces valid, now-richer wrappers. (7) End-to-end: regenerate golden wrapper snapshots and diff to confirm the new accessors/overloads/static members are the ONLY intended deltas. Re-run ts-fx complexity scan to confirm extractImports/extractParameters hotspots are gone.
steps: Add a comprehensive fixtures module (tywrap_ir/tests/fixtures/members.py) exercising @property (get/set), @classmethod, @staticmethod, @cached_property, abstractmethod, @overload, dataclass, TypedDict, NamedTuple, pydantic model, and generics. Snapshot current `python -m tywrap_ir` output to document the baseline gap.

In ir.py: add IRAccessor dataclass; add IRFunction.method_kind ('instance'|'classmethod'|'staticmethod', default 'instance'), IRFunction.is_abstract (bool, default False), IRFunction.overloads (List[IRSignature] or List[IRFunction], default []); add IRClass.accessors (List[IRAccessor], default []).

Rewrite _extract_class member loop (ir.py:481-495): iterate the class dict across MRO (or inspect.classify_class_attrs), classify each value — property -> IRAccessor; classmethod/staticmethod -> unwrap __func__, set method_kind, faithful signature; cached_property -> readonly accessor; abstractmethod -> is_abstract; regular -> instance method. Preserve existing private/__init__ filtering.

Add overload capture: after building each IRFunction, call typing.get_overloads guarded by hasattr(typing,'get_overloads'); attach alternative signatures; on Python 3.10 append a one-time warning to IRModule.warnings noting overloads require 3.11+.
Fix _Final/_Required/_NotRequired substring matching (ir.py:350,512-514) to use typing.get_origin/identity while in the file (F5, cheap and adjacent).

Single-source IR_VERSION: define it once (e.g. tywrap_ir/_version.py or __init__) , import into ir.py/__main__.py/optimized_ir.py defaults; derive __version__ via importlib.metadata with literal fallback; bump IR_VERSION to 0.3.0.

Fix IRCache (optimized_ir.py:88-114): add module-level _MISS sentinel; get() returns _MISS on miss; cached_function checks `is not _MISS`. Replace emoji print(...,file=sys.stderr) calls with the stdlib logging module (Quick Win 10).

On the TS side: extend PythonClass type (types/index.ts) and transformIrToTsModel.mapClass (tywrap.ts:544) to map accessors -> Property (getter/setter/readonly) and propagate method_kind/is_abstract/overloads onto PythonFunction; feed real overloads into generator.ts overload emission (generator.ts:432-494).
Replace the hardcoded TYWRAP_IR_VERSION (tywrap.ts:25) with a single-sourced value and add a CI drift check asserting Python IR_VERSION == TS constant.

Delete src/core/analyzer.ts (PyAnalyzer) as part of the parallel-processor/dead-subsystem removal (B1); remove test/analyzer.test.ts, test/benchmarks/{ir_extraction,code_generation}.bench.ts, test/performance-integration.test.skip.ts analyzer usage, and the barrel export; drop tree-sitter deps from package.json if unused elsewhere.

Add pytest suites: tywrap_ir/tests/test_ir_members.py (assert new member-category shapes against fixtures), tywrap_ir/tests/test_ir_cache.py (None/_MISS regression), and test/python/test_python_bridge_protocol.py (dispatch_request happy + ProtocolError + bytes envelope + size-limit).

Regenerate example wrappers (examples/living-app) and golden snapshots; run npm run check:all, the Python integration suites, and re-run ts-fx; update docs/CLAUDE.md to declare tywrap_ir the single authoritative analyzer and document the new accessor/overload/static-member output and the 3.11+ overload requirement; add a migration note for the removed PyAnalyzer and regenerated wrappers.
